revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|S...'
revision.text:
[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]]
'''クラミツハ'''とは、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
『[[古事記]]』では'''闇御津羽神''''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''闇罔象神'''と表記する。
== 概要 == [[日本神話]]では、[[神産み]]において[[イザナギ|伊弉諾尊]]が[[カグツチ|軻遇突智命]]を斬り殺した際に生まれたとしている。
『古事記』及び『日本書紀』の一書では、御刀の柄に溜まった血が、指の間から漏れ出て、[[淤加美神|闇淤加美神]]（くらおかみのかみ）とともに'''闇御津羽神'''が生まれ、『日本書紀』の一書では迦具土神を斬って生じた三柱の神のうちの一柱が'''高龗神'''（たかおかみのかみ）であるとしている。
また、別の一書では[[闇山祇]]（くらやまつみ）を加えた三柱神が生まれている。
神名の意味で「闇」は谷間を、ミツハは[[ミツハノメ|罔象女神]]の罔象と同意味の[[水]]の神で、「水つ早」と考えられ、「早」は早いことを意味し、始め、端、初期といった意味も持っている。
または「水走」と解して灌漑のための引き水のことを指したものとも、「水つ早」と解して水の出始め（泉、井戸など）のことともされる。 そこから水の出始め現すとし、闇罔象神は峡谷の出始めの水を司る神である。
'''闇龗神'''や'''高龗神'''同様に[[龍]]神である。龍は水や雨を司る神として崇敬される。その為、闇罔象神は[[祈雨]]（きう）、止雨（しう）、[[灌漑]]の神、井戸の神として信仰されている。
賀久留神社（[[静岡県]]浜松市）で闇龗神と共に祀られている。闇龗神や高龗神に比べ祀られる神社は少ない。
== 関連項目 == * [[淤加美神|闇淤加美神]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:くらのみつは}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:龍]] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}}

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]]

'''闇龗神'''や'''高龗神'''同様に[[龍]]神である。龍は水や雨を司る神として崇敬される。その為、闇罔象神は[[祈雨]]（きう）、止雨（しう）、[[灌漑]]の神、井戸の神として信仰されている。

[[zh:闇御津羽神]]

{{特殊文字}}<!--{{lang|zh|龗}}=「雨」の下に3つの「口」と「龍」）--> [[Image:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''クラミツハ'''とは、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
『[[古事記]]』では'''闇御津羽神'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''闇罔象神'''と表記する。
== 神話 == [[日本神話]]では、[[神産み]]において、[[イザナギ|伊弉諾尊]]が[[カグツチ|軻遇突智命]]を斬り殺した際に生まれたとしている。
『古事記』及び『日本書紀』の一書では、御刀の柄に溜まった血が、指の間から漏れ出て、[[淤加美神|闇淤加美神]]（くらおかみのかみ）とともに'''闇御津羽神'''が生まれ、『日本書紀』の一書では迦具土神を斬って生じた三柱の神のうちの一柱が'''高{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''（たかおかみのかみ）であるとしている。
また、別の一書では[[闇山祇]]（くらやまつみ）を加えた三柱神が生まれている。
== 概要 == 神名の意味で「闇」は谷間を、ミツハは[[ミツハノメ|罔象女神]]の罔象と同意味の[[水]]の神で、「水つ早」と考えられ、「早」は早いことを意味し、始め、端、初期といった意味も持っている。
または「水走」と解して灌漑のための引き水のことを指したものとも、「水つ早」と解して水の出始め（[[泉]]、[[井戸]]など）のことともされる。そこから、水の出始めを表すとし、闇罔象神は峡谷の出始めの水を司る神である。
'''闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''や'''高{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''同様に、[[竜|龍]]神である。龍は水や[[雨]]を司る神として崇敬される。その為、闇罔象神は[[雨乞い|祈雨]]（きう）、止雨（しう）、[[灌漑]]の神、井戸の神として信仰されている。
賀久留神社（[[静岡県]][[浜松市]]）で、闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神と共に祀られている。闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神や高{{lang|zh|龗}}神に比べ、祀られる[[神社]]は少ない。

== 神話 == [[日本神話]]では、[[神産み]]において、[[伊邪那岐命]]が[[火之迦具土神]]を斬り殺した際に生まれたとしている。
『古事記』及び『日本書紀』の一書では、迦具土神を斬った御刀の柄に溜まった血が、指の間から漏れ出て、[[淤加美神|闇淤加美神]]（くらおかみのかみ）とともに'''闇御津羽神'''が生まれ、『日本書紀』の一書では軻遇突智命を斬って生じた三柱の神のうちの一柱が'''高{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''（たかおかみのかみ）であるとしている。
また、別の一書では[[闇山津見神|闇山祇]]（くらやまつみ）を加えた三柱神が生まれている。
== 概要 == 「闇」は「暗」で暗がりである谷間を、「ミツハ」は「水早」で水の出始めを意味する。これは[[弥都波能売神]]の罔象（ミツハ）と同意味<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>。
闇御津羽神は峡谷の出始めの水を司る神である。
'''闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''や'''高{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''同様に、[[竜|龍]]神であるともされる。龍は水や[[雨]]を司る神として崇敬される。その為、闇罔象神は[[雨乞い|祈雨]]（きう）、止雨（しう）、[[灌漑]]の神、井戸の神として信仰されている。
賀久留神社（[[静岡県]][[浜松市]]）で、闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神と共に祀られている。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[水神]] * [[淤加美神|闇淤加美神]]

revision.comment: Created page with " # {{ill|Iwa-saku|ja|石折神}}<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Ihasaku</ref> # {{ill|Ne-saku|ja|根折神}} - Minor Star God. # {{ill|Iwa-tsutsu-no-O|ja|石筒之男神}} #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the tip of the sword on the rocks. # {{ill|Mika-Hayahi|ja|甕速日神}} # {{ill|Hi-Hayahi|ja|樋速日神}} # {{ill|Take-Mikagu-tsuchi-no-O|ja|建御雷之男神}}, also known as {{ill|Takefutsu|ja|建布都神}} or {{ill|Toyo-futsu|ja|豊布都..."

revision.text:
# # {{ill|Kura-Mitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神}} #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the handle of the sword.
Also, from the body of Kagutsuchi, the following gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p80-81"/> # {{ill|Masaka-yama-tsumi|ja|正鹿山津見神}}, emerged from Kagutsuchi's head; # {{ill|Odo-yama-tsumi|ja|淤縢山津見神}}, from the chest; # {{ill|Oku-yama-tsumi|ja|奥山津見神}}, from the abdomen; # {{ill|Kura-yama-tsumi|ja|闇山津見神}} (or {{ill|Kura-yama-gi|ja|闇山祇}}), from the genitals; # {{ill|Shigi-yama-tsumi|ja|志藝山津見神}}, from the left arm; # {{ill|Ha-yama-tsumi|ja|羽山津見神}}, from the right arm; # {{ill|Hara-yama-tsumi|ja|原山津見神}}, from the left foot; # {{ill|Toya-ma-tsumi|ja|戸山津見神}} (or {{ill|Toya-ma-gi|ja|戸山祇}}), from the right foot.
===Land of Yomi=== [[File:Green willow and other Japanese fairy tales (1910) (14593788578).jpg|thumb]] Izanagi then decided to bring back Izanami and goes to [[Yomi|Yomi-no-kuni]], the underworld. Crossing the gates to that world, he met Izanami and says to her:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=82–83}}</ref>
{{blockquote|The countries that you and me have created have not been completed yet. Let us return!<ref group="nb">「あなたと一緒に創った国土はまだ完成していません。帰りましょう。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>}}
Izanami replied:
{{blockquote|Too bad you did not come before, for I have eaten in the country of Yomi! [By eating food in the land of Yomi, one ends up stuck being a resident of Yomi. This concept is called, "Yomotsu Hegui (黄泉戸喫)."]<ref>戸部民夫 『日本神話』 29-30頁。</ref> ... however, I will consult with the gods of Yomi. Under no circumstances you may look at me!<ref group="nb">「黄泉の国の食べ物を食べてしまったので、生き返ることはできません」「黄泉神と相談しましょう。お願いですから、私の姿は見ないで下さいね。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>}}
On saying this, Izanami entered the palace of these gods. However, time passed and she did not return and Izanagi began to despair. So he broke one of the tines of his ornamental comb mizura that he wore in the left bun of his hair, lit it in order to light the place and decided to enter the world of dead. He manages to find Izanami but is surprised to see that she lost her beauty and had become a rotting corpse, covered with maggots. Of her body were born the eight Gods of thunder, which were:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
# {{nihongo|'''[[Ō-ikazuchi]]'''|大雷}}, from the head of Izanami; # {{nihongo|'''[[Hono-ikazuchi]]'''|火雷}}, from her chest; # {{nihongo|'''[[Kuro-ikazuchi]]'''|黒雷}}, from her abdomen; # {{nihongo|'''[[Saku-ikazuchi]]'''|折雷}}, from her genitals; # {{nihongo|'''[[Waka-ikazuchi]]'''|若雷}}, from her left arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Tsuchi-ikazuchi]]'''|土雷}}, from her right arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Naru-ikazuchi]]'''|鳴雷}}, from her left foot; # {{nihongo|'''[[Fusu-ikazuchi]]'''|伏雷}}, from her right foot.
Izanagi, shocked, decided to return home, but Izanami was embarrassed by his appearance and commanded the {{nihongo|Yomotsu-shikome|黄泉 丑女|lit. "horrible women from the world of darkness"}} to chase Izanagi. In his flight, he took the head-dress from his head, and threw it to the ground where it turned into a [[bunch of grapes]]. The Yomotsushikome started to eat them but kept chasing the fleeing Izanagi. So he broke the tine of the comb that he wore in his right bun, and as he threw it to the ground it became bamboo shoots, prompting the Yomotsushikome to eat them and enabling Izanagi to flee.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
However, Izanami decided to release the eight gods of thunder and 1,500 warriors from Yomi to continue the pursuit. Izanagi drew and brandished his [[Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]]<ref group="nb" name="grasp"/> sword to continue his flight. As they pursued him, Izanagi reached the {{nihongo|[[Yomotsu-hirasaka]]|黄泉比良坂}}, the slope that descends from the land of the living to Yomi. He took three peaches from a tree that had grown in that place and threw them at his pursuers so that they fled.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanagi commented:
{{blockquote|Assistance to all people when they are tired and face difficulties.<ref group="nb">「人々が困っている時に助けてくれ」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>}}
These peaches were called {{nihongo|Ōkamuzumi|意富加牟豆美命, Ōkamuzumi-no-mikoto}}.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Finally, Izanami persecuted Izanagi, but he lifted a rock that a thousand men could not move and blocked the slope with it. At that moment, their eyes met for the last time.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanami said:
{{blockquote|If you behave in this way, I will strangle and kill one thousand men of your land in one day!<ref group="nb">「私はこれから毎日、一日に千人ずつ殺そう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>}}
Izanagi replied:
{{blockquote|If you do this, I will in one day set up 1,500 parturition houses. So in one day indeed 1,000 men will die and indeed 1,500 are going to be born.<ref group="nb">「それなら私は人間が決して滅びないよう、一日に千五百人生ませよう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>}}
These words justified the circle of life and death in humans. For the same reason, Izanami is also called {{nihongo|'''Yomotsu-ōkami'''|黄泉津大神}} or {{nihongo|'''Chishiki-no-ōkami'''|道敷大神}} and the boulder that covers the entrance to the world of the dead is known as {{nihongo|'''Chika'eshi-no-ōkami'''|道返之大神}} or {{nihongo|'''Yomido-no-ōkami'''|黄泉戸大神}} ō and is today known as {{nihongo|slope of Ifuya|伊賦夜坂, Ifuya-saka}} in [[Izumo, Shimane|Izumo]], [[Shimane Prefecture]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
===Purification of Izanagi=== [[File:Descendants of Izanagi alone-en.svg|thumb|300px|Gods that emerged during the purification of Izanagi.]] Leaving Yomi, Izanagi decided to remove all [[Kegare|uncleanness]] in his body through a purification ceremony (''[[misogi]]'') consisting of a bath in the river at Ahakihara in Tachibana no Ono in Tsukushi. As he stripped his clothes and accessories on the floor the following twelve gods are born:<ref name="kojiki-p84-85">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=84–85}}</ref>
# [[Tsukitatsu funato]] (衝立船戸神 – Post at the Road Bend<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tsukitatsufunato • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tsukitatsufunato/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>) = Chimata no Kami, emerges from the staff. # [[Michi no nagachiha]] (道之長乳歯神 – Long Winding Way Stones<ref>{{Cite web|title=Michinonagachiha • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/michinonagachiha/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>), from the [[Obi (sash)|obi]]. # [[Tokihakashi]] (時量師神 – Time Keeper Loosed<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tokihakashi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tokihakashi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>), from the handbag. # [[Wazurai no ushi]] (和豆良比能宇斯能神 – Master Miasma<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wazurainoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wazurainoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Wazurahinoushi</ref> from cloths. # [[Michi mata]] (道俣神 – Road Fork<ref>{{Cite web|title=Chimata • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/chimata/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>), from the [[hakama]]. # [[Akigui no ushi]] (飽咋之宇斯能神 – Master Filled Full<ref>{{Cite web|title=Akiguinoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/akiguinoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>), from the crown [[Corona (disambiguation)|corona]]. # [[Oki zakaru]] (奥疎神 – Beyond Offshore<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okizakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okizakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu nagisa biko]] (奥津那芸佐毘古神 – Offshore Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu kaibera]] (奥津甲斐弁羅神 – Offshore Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Okitsukahibera</ref> from the armband of the left hand. # [[He zakaru]] (辺疎神 – Beyond Shoreside<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hezakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hezakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu nagisa biko]] (辺津那芸佐毘古神 – Shoreside Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu kaibera]] (辺津甲斐弁羅神 – Shoreside Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>), from the armband of the right hand.
Subsequently Izanagi is stripped of impurities from the land of Yomi. In this moment two gods were born:
# {{ill|Yasomaga-Tsuhi|ja|八十禍津日神}}, male deity # {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}, male deity
Then, shaking off the curse, three gods were born:
# {{ill|Kamuna-obi|ja|神直毘神}}, male deity # {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}, male deity # {{ill|Izunome (kami)|ja|伊豆能売}}, female deity
Then, when washing with water the lower parts of his body, two gods were born;
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Sokotsu-watatsumi]]'''|底津綿津見神}}, genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sokotsutsuno'o]]'''|底筒之男神}}, male deity
When washing the middle of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Nakatsu-watatsumi]]'''|中津綿津見神}}, genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Nakatsutsuno'o]]'''|中筒之男神}}, male deity
Finally, washing the upper part of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Uwatsu-watatsumi]]'''|上津綿津見神}},<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Uhatsuwatatsumi</ref> genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Uwatsutsuno'o]]'''|上筒之男神}}, male deity
The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. The trio of Sokotsutsuno'o, Nakatsutsuno'o and Uhatsutsuno'o make up the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] group of deities, gods of fishing and sea, to whom tribute is paid at [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]].<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
In the last step of the purification ceremony, Izanagi washed his left eye from which the female deity {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'''|天照大御神}} was born; washed his right eye from which the genderless deity and spirit {{nihongo|'''[[Tsukuyomi|Tsukuyomi-no-mikoto]]'''|月読命}} was born; and when washing his nose from which the male deity {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Takehaya-susano'o-no-mikoto]]'''|建速須佐之男命}} = commonly known as Susano'o was born.<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
With these three gods called {{nihongo|[[Mihashira-no-uzu-no-miko]]|三貴子|extra=”Three precious children”}}, Izanagi ordered their investiture. Amaterasu received the mandate to govern [[Takamagahara]] and a necklace of jewels called {{nihongo|Mikuratanano-no-kami|御倉板挙之神}} from Izanagi. Tsukuyomi is mandated to govern over the Dominion of the Night, and Takehaya-susano'o (建速須佐之男命) = Susano'o is to rule the seas.<ref name="kojiki-p86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=86}}</ref>

# # {{ill|Kura-Mitsuha|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇御津羽神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the handle of the sword.
Also, from the body of Kagutsuchi, the following gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p80-81"/> # {{ill|Masaka-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:正鹿山津見神]], emerged from Kagutsuchi's head; # {{ill|Odo-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:淤縢山津見神]], from the chest; # {{ill|Oku-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:奥山津見神]], from the abdomen; # {{ill|Kura-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山津見神]] (or {{ill|Kura-yama-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山祇]]), from the genitals; # {{ill|Shigi-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:志藝山津見神]], from the left arm; # {{ill|Ha-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:羽山津見神]], from the right arm; # {{ill|Hara-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:原山津見神]], from the left foot; # {{ill|Toya-ma-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山津見神]] (or {{ill|Toya-ma-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山祇]]), from the right foot.
===Land of Yomi=== [[File:Green willow and other Japanese fairy tales (1910) (14593788578).jpg|thumb]] Izanagi then decided to bring back Izanami and goes to [[Yomi|Yomi-no-kuni]], the underworld. Crossing the gates to that world, he met Izanami and says to her:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=82–83]]</ref>
{{blockquote|The countries that you and me have created have not been completed yet. Let us return!<ref group="nb">「あなたと一緒に創った国土はまだ完成していません。帰りましょう。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanami replied:
{{blockquote|Too bad you did not come before, for I have eaten in the country of Yomi! [By eating food in the land of Yomi, one ends up stuck being a resident of Yomi. This concept is called, "Yomotsu Hegui (黄泉戸喫)."]<ref>戸部民夫 『日本神話』 29-30頁。</ref> ... however, I will consult with the gods of Yomi. Under no circumstances you may look at me!<ref group="nb">「黄泉の国の食べ物を食べてしまったので、生き返ることはできません」「黄泉神と相談しましょう。お願いですから、私の姿は見ないで下さいね。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
On saying this, Izanami entered the palace of these gods. However, time passed and she did not return and Izanagi began to despair. So he broke one of the tines of his ornamental comb mizura that he wore in the left bun of his hair, lit it in order to light the place and decided to enter the world of dead. He manages to find Izanami but is surprised to see that she lost her beauty and had become a rotting corpse, covered with maggots. Of her body were born the eight Gods of thunder, which were:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
# {{nihongo|'''[[Ō-ikazuchi]]'''|大雷]], from the head of Izanami; # {{nihongo|'''[[Hono-ikazuchi]]'''|火雷]], from her chest; # {{nihongo|'''[[Kuro-ikazuchi]]'''|黒雷]], from her abdomen; # {{nihongo|'''[[Saku-ikazuchi]]'''|折雷]], from her genitals; # {{nihongo|'''[[Waka-ikazuchi]]'''|若雷]], from her left arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Tsuchi-ikazuchi]]'''|土雷]], from her right arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Naru-ikazuchi]]'''|鳴雷]], from her left foot; # {{nihongo|'''[[Fusu-ikazuchi]]'''|伏雷]], from her right foot.
Izanagi, shocked, decided to return home, but Izanami was embarrassed by his appearance and commanded the {{nihongo|Yomotsu-shikome|黄泉 丑女|lit. "horrible women from the world of darkness"]] to chase Izanagi. In his flight, he took the head-dress from his head, and threw it to the ground where it turned into a [[bunch of grapes]]. The Yomotsushikome started to eat them but kept chasing the fleeing Izanagi. So he broke the tine of the comb that he wore in his right bun, and as he threw it to the ground it became bamboo shoots, prompting the Yomotsushikome to eat them and enabling Izanagi to flee.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
However, Izanami decided to release the eight gods of thunder and 1,500 warriors from Yomi to continue the pursuit. Izanagi drew and brandished his [[Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]]<ref group="nb" name="grasp"/> sword to continue his flight. As they pursued him, Izanagi reached the {{nihongo|[[Yomotsu-hirasaka]]|黄泉比良坂]], the slope that descends from the land of the living to Yomi. He took three peaches from a tree that had grown in that place and threw them at his pursuers so that they fled.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanagi commented:
{{blockquote|Assistance to all people when they are tired and face difficulties.<ref group="nb">「人々が困っている時に助けてくれ」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These peaches were called {{nihongo|Ōkamuzumi|意富加牟豆美命, Ōkamuzumi-no-mikoto]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Finally, Izanami persecuted Izanagi, but he lifted a rock that a thousand men could not move and blocked the slope with it. At that moment, their eyes met for the last time.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanami said:
{{blockquote|If you behave in this way, I will strangle and kill one thousand men of your land in one day!<ref group="nb">「私はこれから毎日、一日に千人ずつ殺そう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanagi replied:
{{blockquote|If you do this, I will in one day set up 1,500 parturition houses. So in one day indeed 1,000 men will die and indeed 1,500 are going to be born.<ref group="nb">「それなら私は人間が決して滅びないよう、一日に千五百人生ませよう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These words justified the circle of life and death in humans. For the same reason, Izanami is also called {{nihongo|'''Yomotsu-ōkami'''|黄泉津大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Chishiki-no-ōkami'''|道敷大神]] and the boulder that covers the entrance to the world of the dead is known as {{nihongo|'''Chika'eshi-no-ōkami'''|道返之大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Yomido-no-ōkami'''|黄泉戸大神]] ō and is today known as {{nihongo|slope of Ifuya|伊賦夜坂, Ifuya-saka]] in [[Izumo, Shimane|Izumo]], [[Shimane Prefecture]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
===Purification of Izanagi=== [[File:Descendants of Izanagi alone-en.svg|thumb|300px|Gods that emerged during the purification of Izanagi.]] Leaving Yomi, Izanagi decided to remove all [[Kegare|uncleanness]] in his body through a purification ceremony (''[[misogi]]'') consisting of a bath in the river at Ahakihara in Tachibana no Ono in Tsukushi. As he stripped his clothes and accessories on the floor the following twelve gods are born:<ref name="kojiki-p84-85">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=84–85]]</ref>
# [[Tsukitatsu funato]] (衝立船戸神 – Post at the Road Bend<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tsukitatsufunato • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tsukitatsufunato/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[hakama]]. # [[Akigui no ushi]] (飽咋之宇斯能神 – Master Filled Full<ref>{{Cite web|title=Akiguinoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/akiguinoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the crown [[Corona (disambiguation)|corona]]. # [[Oki zakaru]] (奥疎神 – Beyond Offshore<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okizakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okizakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu nagisa biko]] (奥津那芸佐毘古神 – Offshore Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu kaibera]] (奥津甲斐弁羅神 – Offshore Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Okitsukahibera</ref> from the armband of the left hand. # [[He zakaru]] (辺疎神 – Beyond Shoreside<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hezakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hezakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu nagisa biko]] (辺津那芸佐毘古神 – Shoreside Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - Subsequently Izanagi is stripped of impurities from the land of Yomi. In this moment two gods were born:
# {{ill|Yasomaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:八十禍津日神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大禍津日神]], male deity
Then, shaking off the curse, three gods were born:
# {{ill|Kamuna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:神直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Izunome (kami)|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊豆能売]], female deity
Then, when washing with water the lower parts of his body, two gods were born;
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Sokotsu-watatsumi]]'''|底津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sokotsutsuno'o]]'''|底筒之男神]], male deity
When washing the middle of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Nakatsu-watatsumi]]'''|中津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Nakatsutsuno'o]]'''|中筒之男神]], male deity
Finally, washing the upper part of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Uwatsu-watatsumi]]'''|上津綿津見神]],<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Uhatsuwatatsumi</ref> genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Uwatsutsuno'o]]'''|上筒之男神]], male deity
The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. The trio of Sokotsutsuno'o, Nakatsutsuno'o and Uhatsutsuno'o make up the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] group of deities, gods of fishing and sea, to whom tribute is paid at [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]].<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
In the last step of the purification ceremony, Izanagi washed his left eye from which the female deity {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'''|天照大御神]] was born; washed his right eye from which the genderless deity and spirit {{nihongo|'''[[Tsukuyomi|Tsukuyomi-no-mikoto]]'''|月読命]] was born; and when washing his nose from which the male deity {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Takehaya-susano'o-no-mikoto]]'''|建速須佐之男命]] = commonly known as Susano'o was born.<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
With these three gods called {{nihongo|[[Mihashira-no-uzu-no-miko]]|三貴子|extra=”Three precious children”]], Izanagi ordered their investiture. Amaterasu received the mandate to govern [[Takamagahara]] and a necklace of jewels called {{nihongo|Mikuratanano-no-kami|御倉板挙之神]] from Izanagi. Tsukuyomi is mandated to govern over the Dominion of the Night, and Takehaya-susano'o (建速須佐之男命) = Susano'o is to rule the seas.<ref name="kojiki-p86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=86]]</ref>

[[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Creation myths]] [[Category:Kojiki]] [[Category:Qq]][[en:Kamiumi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E7%94%A3%E3%81%BF]] [[Category:Kamiumi forks]]

# # {{ill|Kura-Mitsuha|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇御津羽神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the handle of the sword.
Also, from the body of Kagutsuchi, the following gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p80-81"/> # {{ill|Masaka-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:正鹿山津見神]], emerged from Kagutsuchi's head; # {{ill|Odo-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:淤縢山津見神]], from the chest; # {{ill|Oku-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:奥山津見神]], from the abdomen; # {{ill|Kura-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山津見神]] (or {{ill|Kura-yama-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山祇]]), from the genitals; # {{ill|Shigi-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:志藝山津見神]], from the left arm; # {{ill|Ha-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:羽山津見神]], from the right arm; # {{ill|Hara-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:原山津見神]], from the left foot; # {{ill|Toya-ma-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山津見神]] (or {{ill|Toya-ma-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山祇]]), from the right foot.
===Land of Yomi=== [[File:Green willow and other Japanese fairy tales (1910) (14593788578).jpg|thumb]] Izanagi then decided to bring back Izanami and goes to [[Yomi|Yomi-no-kuni]], the underworld. Crossing the gates to that world, he met Izanami and says to her:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=82–83]]</ref>
{{blockquote|The countries that you and me have created have not been completed yet. Let us return!<ref group="nb">「あなたと一緒に創った国土はまだ完成していません。帰りましょう。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanami replied:
{{blockquote|Too bad you did not come before, for I have eaten in the country of Yomi! [By eating food in the land of Yomi, one ends up stuck being a resident of Yomi. This concept is called, "Yomotsu Hegui (黄泉戸喫)."]<ref>戸部民夫 『日本神話』 29-30頁。</ref> ... however, I will consult with the gods of Yomi. Under no circumstances you may look at me!<ref group="nb">「黄泉の国の食べ物を食べてしまったので、生き返ることはできません」「黄泉神と相談しましょう。お願いですから、私の姿は見ないで下さいね。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
On saying this, Izanami entered the palace of these gods. However, time passed and she did not return and Izanagi began to despair. So he broke one of the tines of his ornamental comb mizura that he wore in the left bun of his hair, lit it in order to light the place and decided to enter the world of dead. He manages to find Izanami but is surprised to see that she lost her beauty and had become a rotting corpse, covered with maggots. Of her body were born the eight Gods of thunder, which were:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
# {{nihongo|'''[[Ō-ikazuchi]]'''|大雷]], from the head of Izanami; # {{nihongo|'''[[Hono-ikazuchi]]'''|火雷]], from her chest; # {{nihongo|'''[[Kuro-ikazuchi]]'''|黒雷]], from her abdomen; # {{nihongo|'''[[Saku-ikazuchi]]'''|折雷]], from her genitals; # {{nihongo|'''[[Waka-ikazuchi]]'''|若雷]], from her left arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Tsuchi-ikazuchi]]'''|土雷]], from her right arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Naru-ikazuchi]]'''|鳴雷]], from her left foot; # {{nihongo|'''[[Fusu-ikazuchi]]'''|伏雷]], from her right foot.
Izanagi, shocked, decided to return home, but Izanami was embarrassed by his appearance and commanded the {{nihongo|Yomotsu-shikome|黄泉 丑女|lit. "horrible women from the world of darkness"]] to chase Izanagi. In his flight, he took the head-dress from his head, and threw it to the ground where it turned into a [[bunch of grapes]]. The Yomotsushikome started to eat them but kept chasing the fleeing Izanagi. So he broke the tine of the comb that he wore in his right bun, and as he threw it to the ground it became bamboo shoots, prompting the Yomotsushikome to eat them and enabling Izanagi to flee.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
However, Izanami decided to release the eight gods of thunder and 1,500 warriors from Yomi to continue the pursuit. Izanagi drew and brandished his [[Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]]<ref group="nb" name="grasp"/> sword to continue his flight. As they pursued him, Izanagi reached the {{nihongo|[[Yomotsu-hirasaka]]|黄泉比良坂]], the slope that descends from the land of the living to Yomi. He took three peaches from a tree that had grown in that place and threw them at his pursuers so that they fled.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanagi commented:
{{blockquote|Assistance to all people when they are tired and face difficulties.<ref group="nb">「人々が困っている時に助けてくれ」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These peaches were called {{nihongo|Ōkamuzumi|意富加牟豆美命, Ōkamuzumi-no-mikoto]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Finally, Izanami persecuted Izanagi, but he lifted a rock that a thousand men could not move and blocked the slope with it. At that moment, their eyes met for the last time.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanami said:
{{blockquote|If you behave in this way, I will strangle and kill one thousand men of your land in one day!<ref group="nb">「私はこれから毎日、一日に千人ずつ殺そう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanagi replied:
{{blockquote|If you do this, I will in one day set up 1,500 parturition houses. So in one day indeed 1,000 men will die and indeed 1,500 are going to be born.<ref group="nb">「それなら私は人間が決して滅びないよう、一日に千五百人生ませよう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These words justified the circle of life and death in humans. For the same reason, Izanami is also called {{nihongo|'''Yomotsu-ōkami'''|黄泉津大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Chishiki-no-ōkami'''|道敷大神]] and the boulder that covers the entrance to the world of the dead is known as {{nihongo|'''Chika'eshi-no-ōkami'''|道返之大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Yomido-no-ōkami'''|黄泉戸大神]] ō and is today known as {{nihongo|slope of Ifuya|伊賦夜坂, Ifuya-saka]] in [[Izumo, Shimane|Izumo]], [[Shimane Prefecture]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
===Purification of Izanagi=== [[File:Descendants of Izanagi alone-en.svg|thumb|300px|Gods that emerged during the purification of Izanagi.]] Leaving Yomi, Izanagi decided to remove all [[Kegare|uncleanness]] in his body through a purification ceremony (''[[misogi]]'') consisting of a bath in the river at Ahakihara in Tachibana no Ono in Tsukushi. As he stripped his clothes and accessories on the floor the following twelve gods are born:<ref name="kojiki-p84-85">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=84–85]]</ref>
# [[Tsukitatsu funato]] (衝立船戸神 – Post at the Road Bend<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tsukitatsufunato • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tsukitatsufunato/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>) = Chimata no Kami, emerges from the staff. # [[Michi no nagachiha]] (道之長乳歯神 – Long Winding Way Stones<ref>{{Cite web|title=Michinonagachiha • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/michinonagachiha/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[Obi (sash)|obi]]. # [[Tokihakashi]] (時量師神 – Time Keeper Loosed<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tokihakashi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tokihakashi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the handbag. # [[Wazurai no ushi]] (和豆良比能宇斯能神 – Master Miasma<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wazurainoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wazurainoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Wazurahinoushi</ref> from cloths. # [[Michi mata]] (道俣神 – Road Fork<ref>{{Cite web|title=Chimata • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/chimata/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[hakama]]. # [[Akigui no ushi]] (飽咋之宇斯能神 – Master Filled Full<ref>{{Cite web|title=Akiguinoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/akiguinoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the crown [[Corona (disambiguation)|corona]]. # [[Oki zakaru]] (奥疎神 – Beyond Offshore<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okizakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okizakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu nagisa biko]] (奥津那芸佐毘古神 – Offshore Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu kaibera]] (奥津甲斐弁羅神 – Offshore Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Okitsukahibera</ref> from the armband of the left hand. # [[He zakaru]] (辺疎神 – Beyond Shoreside<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hezakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hezakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu nagisa biko]] (辺津那芸佐毘古神 – Shoreside Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu kaibera]] (辺津甲斐弁羅神 – Shoreside Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand.
Subsequently Izanagi is stripped of impurities from the land of Yomi. In this moment two gods were born:
# {{ill|Yasomaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:八十禍津日神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大禍津日神]], male deity
Then, shaking off the curse, three gods were born:
# {{ill|Kamuna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:神直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Izunome (kami)|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊豆能売]], female deity
Then, when washing with water the lower parts of his body, two gods were born;
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Sokotsu-watatsumi]]'''|底津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sokotsutsuno'o]]'''|底筒之男神]], male deity
When washing the middle of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Nakatsu-watatsumi]]'''|中津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Nakatsutsuno'o]]'''|中筒之男神]], male deity
Finally, washing the upper part of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Uwatsu-watatsumi]]'''|上津綿津見神]],<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Uhatsuwatatsumi</ref> genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Uwatsutsuno'o]]'''|上筒之男神]], male deity
The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. The trio of Sokotsutsuno'o, Nakatsutsuno'o and Uhatsutsuno'o make up the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] group of deities, gods of fishing and sea, to whom tribute is paid at [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]].<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
In the last step of the purification ceremony, Izanagi washed his left eye from which the female deity {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'''|天照大御神]] was born; washed his right eye from which the genderless deity and spirit {{nihongo|'''[[Tsukuyomi|Tsukuyomi-no-mikoto]]'''|月読命]] was born; and when washing his nose from which the male deity {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Takehaya-susano'o-no-mikoto]]'''|建速須佐之男命]] = commonly known as Susano'o was born.<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
With these three gods called {{nihongo|[[Mihashira-no-uzu-no-miko]]|三貴子|extra=”Three precious children”]], Izanagi ordered their investiture. Amaterasu received the mandate to govern [[Takamagahara]] and a necklace of jewels called {{nihongo|Mikuratanano-no-kami|御倉板挙之神]] from Izanagi. Tsukuyomi is mandated to govern over the Dominion of the Night, and Takehaya-susano'o (建速須佐之男命) = Susano'o is to rule the seas.<ref name="kojiki-p86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=86]]</ref>

# # {{ill|Kura-Mitsuha|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇御津羽神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the handle of the sword.
Also, from the body of Kagutsuchi, the following gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p80-81"/> # {{ill|Masaka-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:正鹿山津見神]], emerged from Kagutsuchi's head; # {{ill|Odo-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:淤縢山津見神]], from the chest; # {{ill|Oku-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:奥山津見神]], from the abdomen; # {{ill|Kura-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山津見神]] (or {{ill|Kura-yama-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山祇]]), from the genitals; # {{ill|Shigi-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:志藝山津見神]], from the left arm; # {{ill|Ha-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:羽山津見神]], from the right arm; # {{ill|Hara-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:原山津見神]], from the left foot; # {{ill|Toya-ma-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山津見神]] (or {{ill|Toya-ma-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山祇]]), from the right foot.
===Land of Yomi=== [[File:Green willow and other Japanese fairy tales (1910) (14593788578).jpg|thumb]] Izanagi then decided to bring back Izanami and goes to [[Yomi|Yomi-no-kuni]], the underworld. Crossing the gates to that world, he met Izanami and says to her:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=82–83]]</ref>
{{blockquote|The countries that you and me have created have not been completed yet. Let us return!<ref group="nb">「あなたと一緒に創った国土はまだ完成していません。帰りましょう。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanami replied:
{{blockquote|Too bad you did not come before, for I have eaten in the country of Yomi! [By eating food in the land of Yomi, one ends up stuck being a resident of Yomi. This concept is called, "Yomotsu Hegui (黄泉戸喫)."]<ref>戸部民夫 『日本神話』 29-30頁。</ref> ... however, I will consult with the gods of Yomi. Under no circumstances you may look at me!<ref group="nb">「黄泉の国の食べ物を食べてしまったので、生き返ることはできません」「黄泉神と相談しましょう。お願いですから、私の姿は見ないで下さいね。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
On saying this, Izanami entered the palace of these gods. However, time passed and she did not return and Izanagi began to despair. So he broke one of the tines of his ornamental comb mizura that he wore in the left bun of his hair, lit it in order to light the place and decided to enter the world of dead. He manages to find Izanami but is surprised to see that she lost her beauty and had become a rotting corpse, covered with maggots. Of her body were born the eight Gods of thunder, which were:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
# {{nihongo|'''[[Ō-ikazuchi]]'''|大雷]], from the head of Izanami; # {{nihongo|'''[[Hono-ikazuchi]]'''|火雷]], from her chest; # {{nihongo|'''[[Kuro-ikazuchi]]'''|黒雷]], from her abdomen; # {{nihongo|'''[[Saku-ikazuchi]]'''|折雷]], from her genitals; # {{nihongo|'''[[Waka-ikazuchi]]'''|若雷]], from her left arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Tsuchi-ikazuchi]]'''|土雷]], from her right arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Naru-ikazuchi]]'''|鳴雷]], from her left foot; # {{nihongo|'''[[Fusu-ikazuchi]]'''|伏雷]], from her right foot.
Izanagi, shocked, decided to return home, but Izanami was embarrassed by his appearance and commanded the {{nihongo|Yomotsu-shikome|黄泉 丑女|lit. "horrible women from the world of darkness"]] to chase Izanagi. In his flight, he took the head-dress from his head, and threw it to the ground where it turned into a [[bunch of grapes]]. The Yomotsushikome started to eat them but kept chasing the fleeing Izanagi. So he broke the tine of the comb that he wore in his right bun, and as he threw it to the ground it became bamboo shoots, prompting the Yomotsushikome to eat them and enabling Izanagi to flee.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
However, Izanami decided to release the eight gods of thunder and 1,500 warriors from Yomi to continue the pursuit. Izanagi drew and brandished his [[Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]]<ref group="nb" name="grasp"/> sword to continue his flight. As they pursued him, Izanagi reached the {{nihongo|[[Yomotsu-hirasaka]]|黄泉比良坂]], the slope that descends from the land of the living to Yomi. He took three peaches from a tree that had grown in that place and threw them at his pursuers so that they fled.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanagi commented:
{{blockquote|Assistance to all people when they are tired and face difficulties.<ref group="nb">「人々が困っている時に助けてくれ」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These peaches were called {{nihongo|Ōkamuzumi|意富加牟豆美命, Ōkamuzumi-no-mikoto]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Finally, Izanami persecuted Izanagi, but he lifted a rock that a thousand men could not move and blocked the slope with it. At that moment, their eyes met for the last time.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanami said:
{{blockquote|If you behave in this way, I will strangle and kill one thousand men of your land in one day!<ref group="nb">「私はこれから毎日、一日に千人ずつ殺そう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanagi replied:
{{blockquote|If you do this, I will in one day set up 1,500 parturition houses. So in one day indeed 1,000 men will die and indeed 1,500 are going to be born.<ref group="nb">「それなら私は人間が決して滅びないよう、一日に千五百人生ませよう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These words justified the circle of life and death in humans. For the same reason, Izanami is also called {{nihongo|'''Yomotsu-ōkami'''|黄泉津大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Chishiki-no-ōkami'''|道敷大神]] and the boulder that covers the entrance to the world of the dead is known as {{nihongo|'''Chika'eshi-no-ōkami'''|道返之大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Yomido-no-ōkami'''|黄泉戸大神]] ō and is today known as {{nihongo|slope of Ifuya|伊賦夜坂, Ifuya-saka]] in [[Izumo, Shimane|Izumo]], [[Shimane Prefecture]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
===Purification of Izanagi=== [[File:Descendants of Izanagi alone-en.svg|thumb|300px|Gods that emerged during the purification of Izanagi.]] Leaving Yomi, Izanagi decided to remove all [[Kegare|uncleanness]] in his body through a purification ceremony (''[[misogi]]'') consisting of a bath in the river at Ahakihara in Tachibana no Ono in Tsukushi. As he stripped his clothes and accessories on the floor the following twelve gods are born:<ref name="kojiki-p84-85">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=84–85]]</ref>
# [[Tsukitatsu funato]] (衝立船戸神 – Post at the Road Bend<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tsukitatsufunato • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tsukitatsufunato/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>) = Chimata no Kami, emerges from the staff. # [[Michi no nagachiha]] (道之長乳歯神 – Long Winding Way Stones<ref>{{Cite web|title=Michinonagachiha • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/michinonagachiha/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[Obi (sash)|obi]]. # [[Tokihakashi]] (時量師神 – Time Keeper Loosed<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tokihakashi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tokihakashi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the handbag. # [[Wazurai no ushi]] (和豆良比能宇斯能神 – Master Miasma<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wazurainoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wazurainoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Wazurahinoushi</ref> from cloths. # [[Michi mata]] (道俣神 – Road Fork<ref>{{Cite web|title=Chimata • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/chimata/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[hakama]]. # [[Akigui no ushi]] (飽咋之宇斯能神 – Master Filled Full<ref>{{Cite web|title=Akiguinoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/akiguinoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the crown [[Corona (disambiguation)|corona]]. # [[Oki zakaru]] (奥疎神 – Beyond Offshore<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okizakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okizakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu nagisa biko]] (奥津那芸佐毘古神 – Offshore Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu kaibera]] (奥津甲斐弁羅神 – Offshore Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Okitsukahibera</ref> from the armband of the left hand. # [[He zakaru]] (辺疎神 – Beyond Shoreside<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hezakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hezakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu nagisa biko]] (辺津那芸佐毘古神 – Shoreside Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu kaibera]] (辺津甲斐弁羅神 – Shoreside Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand.
Subsequently Izanagi is stripped of impurities from the land of Yomi. In this moment two gods were born:
# {{ill|Yasomaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:八十禍津日神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大禍津日神]], male deity
Then, shaking off the curse, three gods were born:
# {{ill|Kamuna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:神直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Izunome (kami)|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊豆能売]], female deity
Then, when washing with water the lower parts of his body, two gods were born;
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Sokotsu-watatsumi]]'''|底津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sokotsutsuno'o]]'''|底筒之男神]], male deity
When washing the middle of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Nakatsu-watatsumi]]'''|中津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Nakatsutsuno'o]]'''|中筒之男神]], male deity
Finally, washing the upper part of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Uwatsu-watatsumi]]'''|上津綿津見神]],<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Uhatsuwatatsumi</ref> genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Uwatsutsuno'o]]'''|上筒之男神]], male deity
The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. The trio of Sokotsutsuno'o, Nakatsutsuno'o and Uhatsutsuno'o make up the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] group of deities, gods of fishing and sea, to whom tribute is paid at [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]].<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
In the last step of the purification ceremony, Izanagi washed his left eye from which the female deity {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'''|天照大御神]] was born; washed his right eye from which the genderless deity and spirit {{nihongo|'''[[Tsukuyomi|Tsukuyomi-no-mikoto]]'''|月読命]] was born; and when washing his nose from which the male deity {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Takehaya-susano'o-no-mikoto]]'''|建速須佐之男命]] = commonly known as Susano'o was born.<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
With these three gods called {{nihongo|[[Mihashira-no-uzu-no-miko]]|三貴子|extra=”Three precious children”]], Izanagi ordered their investiture. Amaterasu received the mandate to govern [[Takamagahara]] and a necklace of jewels called {{nihongo|Mikuratanano-no-kami|御倉板挙之神]] from Izanagi. Tsukuyomi is mandated to govern over the Dominion of the Night, and Takehaya-susano'o (建速須佐之男命) = Susano'o is to rule the seas.<ref name="kojiki-p86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=86]]</ref>

[[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Creation myths]] [[Category:Kojiki]] [[Category:Qq]][[lld:Kamiumi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E7%94%A3%E3%81%BF]] [[Category:Kamiumi forks]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[zh:%E9%97%87%E5%BE%A1%E6%B4%A5%E7%BE%BD%E7%A5%9E]]

# # {{ill|Kura-Mitsuha|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇御津羽神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the handle of the sword.
Also, from the body of Kagutsuchi, the following gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p80-81"/> # {{ill|Masaka-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:正鹿山津見神]], emerged from Kagutsuchi's head; # {{ill|Odo-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:淤縢山津見神]], from the chest; # {{ill|Oku-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:奥山津見神]], from the abdomen; # {{ill|Kura-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山津見神]] (or {{ill|Kura-yama-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山祇]]), from the genitals; # {{ill|Shigi-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:志藝山津見神]], from the left arm; # {{ill|Ha-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:羽山津見神]], from the right arm; # {{ill|Hara-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:原山津見神]], from the left foot; # {{ill|Toya-ma-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山津見神]] (or {{ill|Toya-ma-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山祇]]), from the right foot.
===Land of Yomi=== [[File:Green willow and other Japanese fairy tales (1910) (14593788578).jpg|thumb]] Izanagi then decided to bring back Izanami and goes to [[Yomi|Yomi-no-kuni]], the underworld. Crossing the gates to that world, he met Izanami and says to her:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=82–83]]</ref>
{{blockquote|The countries that you and me have created have not been completed yet. Let us return!<ref group="nb">「あなたと一緒に創った国土はまだ完成していません。帰りましょう。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanami replied:
{{blockquote|Too bad you did not come before, for I have eaten in the country of Yomi! [By eating food in the land of Yomi, one ends up stuck being a resident of Yomi. This concept is called, "Yomotsu Hegui (黄泉戸喫)."]<ref>戸部民夫 『日本神話』 29-30頁。</ref> ... however, I will consult with the gods of Yomi. Under no circumstances you may look at me!<ref group="nb">「黄泉の国の食べ物を食べてしまったので、生き返ることはできません」「黄泉神と相談しましょう。お願いですから、私の姿は見ないで下さいね。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
On saying this, Izanami entered the palace of these gods. However, time passed and she did not return and Izanagi began to despair. So he broke one of the tines of his ornamental comb mizura that he wore in the left bun of his hair, lit it in order to light the place and decided to enter the world of dead. He manages to find Izanami but is surprised to see that she lost her beauty and had become a rotting corpse, covered with maggots. Of her body were born the eight Gods of thunder, which were:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
# {{nihongo|'''[[Ō-ikazuchi]]'''|大雷]], from the head of Izanami; # {{nihongo|'''[[Hono-ikazuchi]]'''|火雷]], from her chest; # {{nihongo|'''[[Kuro-ikazuchi]]'''|黒雷]], from her abdomen; # {{nihongo|'''[[Saku-ikazuchi]]'''|折雷]], from her genitals; # {{nihongo|'''[[Waka-ikazuchi]]'''|若雷]], from her left arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Tsuchi-ikazuchi]]'''|土雷]], from her right arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Naru-ikazuchi]]'''|鳴雷]], from her left foot; # {{nihongo|'''[[Fusu-ikazuchi]]'''|伏雷]], from her right foot.
Izanagi, shocked, decided to return home, but Izanami was embarrassed by his appearance and commanded the {{nihongo|Yomotsu-shikome|黄泉 丑女|lit. "horrible women from the world of darkness"]] to chase Izanagi. In his flight, he took the head-dress from his head, and threw it to the ground where it turned into a [[bunch of grapes]]. The Yomotsushikome started to eat them but kept chasing the fleeing Izanagi. So he broke the tine of the comb that he wore in his right bun, and as he threw it to the ground it became bamboo shoots, prompting the Yomotsushikome to eat them and enabling Izanagi to flee.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
However, Izanami decided to release the eight gods of thunder and 1,500 warriors from Yomi to continue the pursuit. Izanagi drew and brandished his [[Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]]<ref group="nb" name="grasp"/> sword to continue his flight. As they pursued him, Izanagi reached the {{nihongo|[[Yomotsu-hirasaka]]|黄泉比良坂]], the slope that descends from the land of the living to Yomi. He took three peaches from a tree that had grown in that place and threw them at his pursuers so that they fled.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanagi commented:
{{blockquote|Assistance to all people when they are tired and face difficulties.<ref group="nb">「人々が困っている時に助けてくれ」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These peaches were called {{nihongo|Ōkamuzumi|意富加牟豆美命, Ōkamuzumi-no-mikoto]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Finally, Izanami persecuted Izanagi, but he lifted a rock that a thousand men could not move and blocked the slope with it. At that moment, their eyes met for the last time.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanami said:
{{blockquote|If you behave in this way, I will strangle and kill one thousand men of your land in one day!<ref group="nb">「私はこれから毎日、一日に千人ずつ殺そう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanagi replied:
{{blockquote|If you do this, I will in one day set up 1,500 parturition houses. So in one day indeed 1,000 men will die and indeed 1,500 are going to be born.<ref group="nb">「それなら私は人間が決して滅びないよう、一日に千五百人生ませよう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These words justified the circle of life and death in humans. For the same reason, Izanami is also called {{nihongo|'''Yomotsu-ōkami'''|黄泉津大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Chishiki-no-ōkami'''|道敷大神]] and the boulder that covers the entrance to the world of the dead is known as {{nihongo|'''Chika'eshi-no-ōkami'''|道返之大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Yomido-no-ōkami'''|黄泉戸大神]] ō and is today known as {{nihongo|slope of Ifuya|伊賦夜坂, Ifuya-saka]] in [[Izumo, Shimane|Izumo]], [[Shimane Prefecture]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
===Purification of Izanagi=== [[File:Descendants of Izanagi alone-en.svg|thumb|300px|Gods that emerged during the purification of Izanagi.]] Leaving Yomi, Izanagi decided to remove all [[Kegare|uncleanness]] in his body through a purification ceremony (''[[misogi]]'') consisting of a bath in the river at Ahakihara in Tachibana no Ono in Tsukushi. As he stripped his clothes and accessories on the floor the following twelve gods are born:<ref name="kojiki-p84-85">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=84–85]]</ref>
# [[Tsukitatsu funato]] (衝立船戸神 – Post at the Road Bend<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tsukitatsufunato • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tsukitatsufunato/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>) = Chimata no Kami, emerges from the staff. # [[Michi no nagachiha]] (道之長乳歯神 – Long Winding Way Stones<ref>{{Cite web|title=Michinonagachiha • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/michinonagachiha/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[hakama]]. # [[Akigui no ushi]] (飽咋之宇斯能神 – Master Filled Full<ref>{{Cite web|title=Akiguinoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/akiguinoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the crown [[Corona (disambiguation)|corona]]. # [[Oki zakaru]] (奥疎神 – Beyond Offshore<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okizakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okizakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu nagisa biko]] (奥津那芸佐毘古神 – Offshore Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu kaibera]] (奥津甲斐弁羅神 – Offshore Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Okitsukahibera</ref> from the armband of the left hand. # [[He zakaru]] (辺疎神 – Beyond Shoreside<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hezakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hezakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu nagisa biko]] (辺津那芸佐毘古神 – Shoreside Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand.
Subsequently Izanagi is stripped of impurities from the land of Yomi. In this moment two gods were born:
# {{ill|Yasomaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:八十禍津日神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大禍津日神]], male deity
Then, shaking off the curse, three gods were born:
# {{ill|Kamuna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:神直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Izunome (kami)|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊豆能売]], female deity
Then, when washing with water the lower parts of his body, two gods were born;
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Sokotsu-watatsumi]]'''|底津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sokotsutsuno'o]]'''|底筒之男神]], male deity
When washing the middle of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Nakatsu-watatsumi]]'''|中津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Nakatsutsuno'o]]'''|中筒之男神]], male deity
Finally, washing the upper part of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Uwatsu-watatsumi]]'''|上津綿津見神]],<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Uhatsuwatatsumi</ref> genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Uwatsutsuno'o]]'''|上筒之男神]], male deity
The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. The trio of Sokotsutsuno'o, Nakatsutsuno'o and Uhatsutsuno'o make up the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] group of deities, gods of fishing and sea, to whom tribute is paid at [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]].<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
In the last step of the purification ceremony, Izanagi washed his left eye from which the female deity {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'''|天照大御神]] was born; washed his right eye from which the genderless deity and spirit {{nihongo|'''[[Tsukuyomi|Tsukuyomi-no-mikoto]]'''|月読命]] was born; and when washing his nose from which the male deity {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Takehaya-susano'o-no-mikoto]]'''|建速須佐之男命]] = commonly known as Susano'o was born.<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
With these three gods called {{nihongo|[[Mihashira-no-uzu-no-miko]]|三貴子|extra=”Three precious children”]], Izanagi ordered their investiture. Amaterasu received the mandate to govern [[Takamagahara]] and a necklace of jewels called {{nihongo|Mikuratanano-no-kami|御倉板挙之神]] from Izanagi. Tsukuyomi is mandated to govern over the Dominion of the Night, and Takehaya-susano'o (建速須佐之男命) = Susano'o is to rule the seas.<ref name="kojiki-p86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=86]]</ref>

{{特殊文字}}<!--{{lang|zh|龗}}=「雨」の下に3つの「口」と「龍」--> [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''クラミツハ'''とは、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
『[[古事記]]』では'''闇御津羽神'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''闇罔象神'''と表記する。
== 神話 == [[日本神話]]では、[[神産み]]において、[[伊邪那岐命]]が[[火之迦具土神]]を斬り殺した際に生まれたとしている。
『古事記』及び『日本書紀』の一書では、迦具土神を斬った御刀の柄に溜まった血が、指の間から漏れ出て、[[淤加美神|闇淤加美神]]（くらおかみのかみ）とともに'''闇御津羽神'''が生まれ、『日本書紀』の一書では軻遇突智命を斬って生じた三柱の神のうちの一柱が'''高{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''（たかおかみのかみ）であるとしている。
また、別の一書では[[闇山津見神|闇山祇]]（くらやまつみ）を加えた三柱神が生まれている。
== 概要 == 「闇」は「暗」で暗がりである谷間を、「ミツハ」は「水早」で水の出始めを意味する。これは[[弥都波能売神]]の罔象（ミツハ）と同意味<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>。
闇御津羽神は峡谷の出始めの水を司る神である。
'''闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''や'''高{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''同様に、[[竜|龍]]神であるともされる。龍は水や[[雨]]を司る神として崇敬される。その為、闇罔象神は[[雨乞い|祈雨]]（きう）、止雨（しう）、[[灌漑]]の神、井戸の神として信仰されている。
賀久留神社（[[静岡県]][[浜松市]]）で、闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神と共に祀られている。

revision.text:
{{特殊文字}}<!--{{lang|zh|龗}}=「雨」の下に3つの「口」と「龍」--> [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''クラミツハ'''とは、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
『[[古事記]]』では'''闇御津羽神'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''闇罔象神'''と表記する。
== 神話 == [[日本神話]]では、[[神産み]]において、[[伊邪那岐命]]が[[火之迦具土神]]を斬り殺した際に生まれたとしている。
『古事記』及び『日本書紀』の一書では、迦具土神を斬った御刀の柄に溜まった血が、指の間から漏れ出て、[[淤加美神|闇淤加美神]]（くらおかみのかみ）とともに'''闇御津羽神'''が生まれ、『日本書紀』の一書では軻遇突智命を斬って生じた三柱の神のうちの一柱が'''高{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''（たかおかみのかみ）であるとしている。
また、別の一書では[[闇山津見神|闇山祇]]（くらやまつみ）を加えた三柱神が生まれている。
== 概要 == 「闇」は「暗」で暗がりである谷間を、「ミツハ」は「水早」で水の出始めを意味する。これは[[弥都波能売神]]の罔象（ミツハ）と同意味<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>。
闇御津羽神は峡谷の出始めの水を司る神である。
'''闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''や'''高{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''同様に、[[竜|龍]]神であるともされる。龍は水や[[雨]]を司る神として崇敬される。その為、闇罔象神は[[雨乞い|祈雨]]（きう）、止雨（しう）、[[灌漑]]の神、井戸の神として信仰されている。
賀久留神社（[[静岡県]][[浜松市]]）で、闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神と共に祀られている。

また、別の一書では[[闇山津見神|闇山祇]]（くらやまつみ）を加えた三柱神が生まれている。

闇御津羽神は峡谷の出始めの水を司る神である。
'''闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''や'''高{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''同様に、[[竜|龍]]神であるともされる。龍は水や[[雨]]を司る神として崇敬される。その為、闇罔象神は[[雨乞い|祈雨]]（きう）、止雨（しう）、[[灌漑]]の神、井戸の神として信仰されている。
賀久留神社（[[静岡県]][[浜松市]]）で、闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神と共に祀られている。

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Generation of Gods (the gods born of Izanagi and Izanami) [[media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|Display as SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Kuramitsuha''' (闇御津羽神) is a {{ill|God (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)|lt=God|lt_ja=神}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
In {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, it is written as '''闇御津羽神''', and in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as '''闇罔象神'''.
== Mythology == In {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, during the {{ill|Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み|lt=Divine Birth|lt_ja=神産み}}, it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to {{ill|Yamiokami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Yami Okami|lt_ja=闇淤加美神}} along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a {{ill|Dragon deity|ja|竜|lt=Dragon god|lt_ja=龍}}. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and {{ill|Rain|ja|雨}}. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of {{ill|Rainmaking|ja|雨乞い|lt=Pray for rain|lt_ja=祈雨}}, rain cessation (しう), {{ill|Irrigation|ja|灌漑}}, and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} and {{ill|Hamamatsu City|ja|浜松市}}), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.

== Mythology == In {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, during the {{ill|Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み|lt=Divine Birth|lt_ja=神産み}}, it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to {{ill|Yamiokami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Yami Okami|lt_ja=闇淤加美神}} along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇}} (くらやまつみ).

Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.

Dragons are revered as deities governing water and {{ill|Rain|ja|雨}}. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of {{ill|Rainmaking|ja|雨乞い|lt=Pray for rain|lt_ja=祈雨}}, rain cessation (しう), {{ill|Irrigation|ja|灌漑}}, and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} and {{ill|Hamamatsu City|ja|浜松市}}), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Generation of Gods (the gods born of Izanagi and Izanami) [[media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|Display as SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Kuramitsuha''' (闇御津羽神) is a {{ill|God (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)|lt=God|lt_ja=神}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
In {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, it is written as '''闇御津羽神''', and in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as '''闇罔象神'''.
== Mythology == In {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, during the {{ill|Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み|lt=Divine Birth|lt_ja=神産み}}, it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to {{ill|Yamiokami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Yami Okami|lt_ja=闇淤加美神}} along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a {{ill|Dragon deity|ja|竜|lt=Dragon god|lt_ja=龍}}. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and {{ill|Rain|ja|雨}}. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of {{ill|Rainmaking|ja|雨乞い|lt=Pray for rain|lt_ja=祈雨}}, rain cessation (しう), {{ill|Irrigation|ja|灌漑}}, and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}} and {{ill|Hamamatsu City|ja|浜松市}}), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.

Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of {{ill|Rainmaking|ja|雨乞い|lt=Pray for rain|lt_ja=祈雨|12=draft}}, rain cessation (しう), {{ill|Irrigation|ja|灌漑|12=draft}}, and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hamamatsu City|ja|浜松市|12=draft}}), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.

revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Generation of Gods (the gods born of Izanagi and Izanami) [[media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|Display as SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Kuramitsuha''' (闇御津羽神) is a {{ill|God (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)|lt=God|lt_ja=神|12=draft}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}.
In {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, it is written as '''闇御津羽神''', and in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, as '''闇罔象神'''.
== Mythology == In {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}, during the {{ill|Birth of the Gods|ja|神産み|lt=Divine Birth|lt_ja=神産み|12=draft}}, it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神|12=draft}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to {{ill|Yamiokami|ja|淤加美神|lt=Yami Okami|lt_ja=闇淤加美神|12=draft}} along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|12=draft}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|12=draft}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a {{ill|Dragon deity|ja|竜|lt=Dragon god|lt_ja=龍|12=draft}}. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and {{ill|Rain|ja|雨|12=draft}}. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of {{ill|Rainmaking|ja|雨乞い|lt=Pray for rain|lt_ja=祈雨|12=draft}}, rain cessation (しう), {{ill|Irrigation|ja|灌漑|12=draft}}, and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hamamatsu City|ja|浜松市|12=draft}}), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Japanese Gods]]
Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Water Gods]]
Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Generation of Gods (the gods born of Izanagi and Izanami) ]] '''Kuramitsuha''' (闇御津羽神) is a [[:en:Kami|God]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
In [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], it is written as '''闇御津羽神''', and in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as '''闇罔象神'''.
== Mythology == In [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Divine Birth]], it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi no Mikoto|qq=}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi|qq=}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to [[:en:Kuraokami|Yami Okami]] along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|qq=}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|qq=}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a [[:en:Dragon|Dragon god]]. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and [[:en:Rain|Rain]]. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of [[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Pray for rain]], rain cessation (しう), [[:en:Irrigation|Irrigation]], and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu City]]), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.
== References == {{Reflist}}
=== Related Topics === * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese Gods]] * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * [[:en:Kuraokami|Yami Okami]]

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Generation of Gods (the gods born of Izanagi and Izanami) ]] '''Kuramitsuha''' (闇御津羽神) is a [[:en:Kami|God]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
In [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], it is written as '''闇御津羽神''', and in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as '''闇罔象神'''.
== Mythology == In [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Divine Birth]], it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to [[:en:Kuraokami|Yami Okami]] along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a [[:en:Dragon|Dragon god]]. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and [[:en:Rain|Rain]]. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of [[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Pray for rain]], rain cessation (しう), [[:en:Irrigation|Irrigation]], and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu City]]), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.
