== Mythology == In [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Divine Birth]], it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to [[:en:Kuraokami|Yami Okami]] along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a [[:en:Dragon|Dragon god]]. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and [[:en:Rain|Rain]]. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of [[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Pray for rain]], rain cessation (しう), [[:en:Irrigation|Irrigation]], and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu City]]), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Generation of Gods (the gods born of Izanagi and Izanami) ]] '''Kuramitsuha''' (闇御津羽神) is a [[:en:Kami|God]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
In [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], it is written as '''闇御津羽神''', and in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as '''闇罔象神'''.
== Mythology == In [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Divine Birth]], it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ|1=Kagu-tsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1640658}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to [[:en:Kuraokami|Yami Okami]] along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a [[:en:Dragon|Dragon god]]. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and [[:en:Rain|Rain]]. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of [[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Pray for rain]], rain cessation (しう), [[:en:Irrigation|Irrigation]], and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu City]]), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: 22 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:クラミツハ up to rev 100167181
revision.text:
{{特殊文字}}<!--{{lang|zh|龗}}=「雨」の下に3つの「口」と「龍」--> [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''クラミツハ'''とは、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
『[[古事記]]』では'''闇御津羽神'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''闇罔象神'''と表記する。
== 神話 == [[日本神話]]では、[[神産み]]において、[[伊邪那岐命]]が[[火之迦具土神]]を斬り殺した際に生まれたとしている。
『古事記』及び『日本書紀』の一書では、迦具土神を斬った御刀の柄に溜まった血が、指の間から漏れ出て、[[淤加美神|闇淤加美神]]（くらおかみのかみ）とともに'''闇御津羽神'''が生まれ、『日本書紀』の一書では軻遇突智命を斬って生じた三柱の神のうちの一柱が'''高{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''（たかおかみのかみ）であるとしている。
また、別の一書では[[闇山津見神|闇山祇]]（くらやまつみ）を加えた三柱神が生まれている。
== 概要 == 「闇」は「暗」で暗がりである谷間を、「ミツハ」は「水早」で水の出始めを意味する。これは[[弥都波能売神]]の罔象（ミツハ）と同意味<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>。
闇御津羽神は峡谷の出始めの水を司る神である。
'''闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''や'''高{{lang|zh|龗}}神'''同様に、[[竜|龍]]神であるともされる。龍は水や[[雨]]を司る神として崇敬される。その為、闇罔象神は[[雨乞い|祈雨]]（きう）、止雨（しう）、[[灌漑]]の神、井戸の神として信仰されている。
賀久留神社（[[静岡県]][[浜松市]]）で、闇{{lang|zh|龗}}神と共に祀られている。
== 参考文献 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[水神]] * [[淤加美神|闇淤加美神]]
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くらみつはのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:日本の竜]]

In one version from the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to [[:en:Kuraokami|Yami Okami]] along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a [[:en:Dragon|Dragon god]]. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and [[:en:Rain|Rain]]. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of [[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Pray for rain]], rain cessation (しう), [[:en:Irrigation|Irrigation]], and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu City]]), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:クラミツハ]][[zh:闇御津羽神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Generation of Gods (the gods born of Izanagi and Izanami) ]] '''Kuramitsuha''' (闇御津羽神) is a [[:en:Kami|God]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
In [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], it is written as '''闇御津羽神''', and in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as '''闇罔象神'''.
== Mythology == In [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Divine Birth]], it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ|1=Kagu-tsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1640658}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to [[:en:Kuraokami|Yami Okami]] along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a [[:en:Dragon|Dragon god]]. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and [[:en:Rain|Rain]]. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of [[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Pray for rain]], rain cessation (しう), [[:en:Irrigation|Irrigation]], and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu City]]), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.
== References == {{Reflist}}
=== Related Topics === * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese Gods]] * [[:en:Suijin|water god]] * [[:en:Kuraokami|Yami Okami]]

[[Category:Completion tier 1]]

In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (くらやまつみ).

This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>

Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a [[:en:Dragon|Dragon god]]. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and [[:en:Rain|Rain]]. At Kakuru Shrine (located in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu City]]), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.

Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a [[:en:Dragon|Dragon god]]. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and [[:en:Rain|Rain]]. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of [[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Pray for rain]], rain cessation (しう), [[:en:Irrigation|Irrigation]], and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu City]]), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:クラミツハ]][[zh:闇御津羽神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Generation of Gods (the gods born of Izanagi and Izanami) ]] '''Kuramitsuha''' (闇御津羽神) is a [[:en:Kami|God]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
In [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], it is written as '''闇御津羽神''', and in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as '''闇罔象神'''.
== Mythology == In [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Divine Birth]], it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ|1=Kagu-tsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1640658}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to [[:en:Kuraokami|Yami Okami]] along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a [[:en:Dragon|Dragon god]]. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and [[:en:Rain|Rain]]. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of [[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Pray for rain]], rain cessation (しう), [[:en:Irrigation|Irrigation]], and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu City]]), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:クラミツハ]][[zh:闇御津羽神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:クラミツハ]][[zh:闇御津羽神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:クラミツハ]][[zh:闇御津羽神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Generation of Gods (the gods born of Izanagi and Izanami) ]] '''Kuramitsuha''' (闇御津羽神) is a [[:en:Kami|God]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
In [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], it is written as '''闇御津羽神''', and in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as '''闇罔象神'''.
== Mythology == In [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], during the [[:en:Kamiumi|Divine Birth]], it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ|1=Kagu-tsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1640658}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to [[:en:Kuraokami|Yami Okami]] along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a [[:en:Dragon|Dragon god]]. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and [[:en:Rain|Rain]]. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of [[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Pray for rain]], rain cessation (しう), [[:en:Irrigation|Irrigation]], and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu City]]), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.
== References == {{Reflist}}

In another version of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (くらやまつみ).

This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>

Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a [[:en:Dragon|Dragon god]]. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and [[:en:Rain|Rain]]. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of [[:en:Rainmaking (ritual)|Pray for rain]], rain cessation (しう), [[:en:Irrigation|Irrigation]], and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu City]]), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Generation of Gods (the gods born of Izanagi and Izanami) ]] '''Kuramitsuha''' (闇御津羽神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=God}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.
In {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, it is written as '''闇御津羽神''', and in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, as '''闇罔象神'''.
== Mythology == In {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}, during the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Divine Birth}}, it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ|1=Kagu-tsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1640658}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Yami Okami}} along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a {{ill|Dragon|en|Dragon|WD=Q7559|lt=Dragon god}}. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and {{ill|Rain|en|Rain|WD=Q7925|lt=Rain}}. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of {{ill|Rainmaking (ritual)|en|Rainmaking (ritual)|WD=Q477738|lt=Pray for rain}}, rain cessation (しう), {{ill|Irrigation|en|Irrigation|WD=Q11453|lt=Irrigation}}, and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}} and {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu City}}), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.
== References == {{Reflist}}
=== Related Topics === * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese Gods}} * {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god}} * {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Yami Okami}}

=== Related Topics === * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese Gods|1=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}} * {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Yami Okami|1=Kuraokami}}
{{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}}

[[ja:クラミツハ]] {{translated page|ja|クラミツハ|version=100167181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11299063}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:クラミツハ]][[zh:闇御津羽神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:クラミツハ]][[zh:闇御津羽神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:クラミツハ]][[zh:闇御津羽神]]
[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Generation of Gods (the gods born of Izanagi and Izanami) ]] '''Kuramitsuha''' (闇御津羽神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=God|1=Kami}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}.
In {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, it is written as '''闇御津羽神''', and in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, as '''闇罔象神'''.
== Mythology == In {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}, during the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=Divine Birth|1=Kamiumi}}, it is said that {{ill|Izanagi no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} slew {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ|1=Kagu-tsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1640658}}, and thus Kuramitsuha was born.
In one version from the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, the blood that accumulated on the hilt of the sword used to cut Kagutsuchi—leaking from between the fingers—gave rise to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Yami Okami|1=Kuraokami}} along with the birth of '''闇御津羽神'''. In another version of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, one of the three deities produced by the slaying of 軻遇突智命 is identified as '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' (たかおかみのかみ).
In another account, three deities are born, including {{ill|Kurayamatsumi|ja|闇山津見神|lt=Kurayama-tsumi|lt_ja=闇山祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (くらやまつみ).
== Overview == The term "Yami" (闇) means "dark" and refers to a dim valley, while "Mitsuha" (ミツハ) derives from "hayai mizu" (水早) and signifies the initial flow of water. This is synonymous in meaning with the "Mitsuha" of {{ill|Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja|弥都波能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatohanamonomi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}.<ref>新潮社古典集成 古事記</ref>
Yamimitsuhaga no Kami is the deity presiding over the emerging waters of a gorge.
Like '''Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神''' and '''Taka{{lang|zh|龗}}神''', it is also considered a {{ill|Dragon|en|Dragon|WD=Q7559|lt=Dragon god|1=Dragon}}. Dragons are revered as deities governing water and {{ill|Rain|en|Rain|WD=Q7925|lt=Rain|1=Rain}}. Consequently, Yamimōshōjin is venerated as the deity of {{ill|Rainmaking (ritual)|en|Rainmaking (ritual)|WD=Q477738|lt=Pray for rain|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}}, rain cessation (しう), {{ill|Irrigation|en|Irrigation|WD=Q11453|lt=Irrigation|1=Irrigation}}, and wells.
At Kakuru Shrine (located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} and {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu City|1=Hamamatsu}}), it is enshrined together with Yami{{lang|zh|龗}}神.
== References == {{Reflist}}
=== Related Topics === * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese Gods|1=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}} * {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Yami Okami|1=Kuraokami}}
{{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}}
